   146→        # Step 3: Scan DOM for elements
   147→        elements = await page.evaluate(DISCOVER_ELEMENTS_JS)
   148→
   149→        if not elements:
   150→            console.print(f"[yellow]No interactive elements found (attempt {attempt}/{MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES}).[/yellow]")
   151→            if attempt < MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES:
   152→                console.print("[dim]Make sure the chat page is fully loaded and try again.[/dim]")
   153→                continue
   154→            else:
   155→                console.print("[red]Giving up after max retries. Selector discovery failed.[/red]")
   156→                return discovered
   157→
   158→        # Step 4: Display discovered elements
   159→        input_elements = [e for e in elements if e['type'] == 'input']
   160→        button_elements = [e for e in elements if e['type'] == 'button']
   161→
   162→        # Select message input
   163→        if input_elements:
   164→            console.print("\n[bold cyan]Input elements found:[/bold cyan]")
   165→            for i, el in enumerate(input_elements):
   166→                label = el.get('text') or el.get('classes', '') or el['tag']
   167→                console.print(f"  [{i}] {el['tag']} - {label} (selector: {el['selector']})")
   168→
   169→            choice = await loop.run_in_executor(
   170→                None,
   171→                lambda: input(f"\nSelect message input [0-{len(input_elements)-1}] (default 0): ").strip()
   172→            )
   173→            idx = int(choice) if choice.isdigit() and int(choice) < len(input_elements) else 0
   174→            discovered.message_input = input_elements[idx]['selector']
   175→            console.print(f"[green]Message input:[/green] {discovered.message_input}")
   176→        else:
   177→            console.print("[yellow]No input elements found.[/yellow]")
   178→            if attempt < MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES:
   179→                continue
   180→            return discovered
   181→
   182→        # Select send button
   183→        if button_elements:
   184→            console.print("\n[bold cyan]Button elements found:[/bold cyan]")
   185→            for i, el in enumerate(button_elements):
   186→                label = el.get('text') or el.get('classes', '') or el['tag']
   187→                console.print(f"  [{i}] {el['tag']} - \"{label}\" (selector: {el['selector']})")
   188→
   189→            choice = await loop.run_in_executor(
   190→                None,
   191→                lambda: input(f"\nSelect send button [0-{len(button_elements)-1}]: ").strip()
   192→            )
   193→            if choice.isdigit() and int(choice) < len(button_elements):
   194→                discovered.send_button = button_elements[int(choice)]['selector']
   195→                console.print(f"[green]Send button:[/green] {discovered.send_button}")
   196→            else:
   197→                console.print("[yellow]No send button selected.[/yellow]")
   198→
   199→        # Step 5: Ask about chat button
   200→        needs_chat = await loop.run_in_executor(
   201→            None,
   202→            lambda: input("\nDoes reaching the chat page require clicking a button first? [y/N]: ").strip().lower()
   203→        )
   204→
   205→        if needs_chat == 'y':
   206→            console.print("\n[bold cyan]Select the chat button from the button list above:[/bold cyan]")
   207→            choice = await loop.run_in_executor(
   208→                None,
   209→                lambda: input(f"Chat button index [0-{len(button_elements)-1}]: ").strip()
   210→            )
   211→            if choice.isdigit() and int(choice) < len(button_elements):
   212→                discovered.chat_button = button_elements[int(choice)]['selector']
   213→                console.print(f"[green]Chat button:[/green] {discovered.chat_button}")
   214→        else:
   215→            discovered.skip_chat_button = True
   216→
   217→        # Mark as populated if we have at least message_input
   218→        if discovered.message_input:
   219→            discovered.is_populated = True
   220→            console.print(Panel(
   221→                "[green]Selector discovery complete! These selectors will be reused for remaining candidates.[/green]",
   222→                border_style="green",
   223→            ))
   224→            break
   225→
   226→    return discovered
   227→
   228→
   229→async def apply_discovered_selectors(
   230→    page: Page,
   231→    maimai_id: str,
   232→    discovered: DiscoveredSelectors,
   233→    content: str,
   234→) -> bool:
   235→    """
   236→    Send a message using previously discovered selectors.
   237→
   238→    Args:
   239→        page: Playwright page instance
   240→        maimai_id: Candidate's Maimai ID
   241→        discovered: Previously discovered selectors
   242→        content: Message content to send
   243→
   244→    Returns:
   245→        True if message sent successfully
   246→    """
   247→    try:
   248→        # Navigate to chat URL if pattern is available
   249→        if discovered.chat_url_pattern:
   250→            chat_url = discovered.chat_url_pattern.format(maimai_id=maimai_id)
   251→            await page.goto(chat_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   252→            await async_random_delay('page_load')
   253→
   254→        # Click chat button if needed
   255→        if not discovered.skip_chat_button and discovered.chat_button:
   256→            try:
   257→                await human_click(page, discovered.chat_button)
   258→                await async_random_delay('action')
   259→            except Exception as e:
   260→                logger.warning(f"Failed to click chat button: {e}")
   261→                return False
   262→
   263→        # Type message
   264→        if discovered.message_input:
   265→            try:
   266→                await page.wait_for_selector(discovered.message_input, timeout=10000)
   267→                await human_type(page, discovered.message_input, content)
   268→                await async_random_delay('action')
   269→            except Exception as e:
   270→                logger.warning(f"Failed to type message: {e}")
   271→                return False
   272→
   273→        # Click send
   274→        if discovered.send_button:
   275→            try:
   276→                await human_click(page, discovered.send_button)
   277→                await async_random_delay('action')
   278→
   279→                # Verify message was sent
   280→                from src.modules.message import _verify_message_sent
   281→                verified = await _verify_message_sent(page, content)
   282→                if not verified:
   283→                    logger.warning("Message send via discovered selectors could not be verified")
   284→                    return False
   285→                logger.debug("Message send via discovered selectors verified")
   286→                return True
   287→            except Exception as e:
   288→                logger.warning(f"Failed to click send: {e}")
   289→                return False
   290→
   291→        logger.warning("No send button selector available")
   292→        return False
   293→
   294→    except Exception as e:
   295→        logger.error(f"Error applying discovered selectors: {e}")
   296→        return False
   297→

1→"""Configuration loader and manager."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→from pathlib import Path
     5→from typing import Any, Dict
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→
     9→from src.exceptions.errors import ConfigurationError
    10→
    11→
    12→class Config:
    13→    """Configuration manager for Maimai Assistant."""
    14→
    15→    def __init__(self, config_dir: str = None):
    16→        if config_dir is None:
    17→            # Resolve relative to project root (3 levels up from this file)
    18→            config_dir = str(Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "config")
    19→        self.config_dir = Path(config_dir)
    20→        self._settings: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    21→        self._selectors: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    22→        self._message_templates: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    23→
    24→        self._load_all()
    25→
    26→    def _load_json(self, filename: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    27→        """Load JSON configuration file."""
    28→        filepath = self.config_dir / filename
    29→        try:
    30→            with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    31→                return json.load(f)
    32→        except FileNotFoundError:
    33→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Configuration file not found: {filepath}")
    34→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    35→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid JSON in {filepath}: {e}")
    36→
    37→    def _load_all(self):
    38→        """Load all configuration files."""
    39→        logger.info("Loading configuration files...")
    40→        self._settings = self._load_json("settings.json")
    41→        self._selectors = self._load_json("selectors.json")
    42→        self._message_templates = self._load_json("message_templates.json")
    43→        logger.info("Configuration loaded successfully")
    44→
    45→    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    46→        """Get setting by dot-notation key (e.g., 'browser.headless')."""
    47→        keys = key.split('.')
    48→        value = self._settings
    49→
    50→        for k in keys:
    51→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    52→                value = value.get(k)
    53→                if value is None:
    54→                    return default
    55→            else:
    56→                return default
    57→
    58→        return value
    59→
    60→    def get_selector(self, selector_path: str) -> str:
    61→        """Get selector by dot-notation path (e.g., 'search.keyword_input')."""
    62→        keys = selector_path.split('.')
    63→        value = self._selectors.get('selectors', {})
    64→
    65→        for k in keys:
    66→            if isinstance(value, dict):
    67→                value = value.get(k)
    68→                if value is None:
    69→                    raise ConfigurationError(f"Selector not found: {selector_path}")
    70→            else:
    71→                raise ConfigurationError(f"Invalid selector path: {selector_path}")
    72→
    73→        return value
    74→
    75→    def get_template(self, template_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    76→        """Get message template by name."""
    77→        template = self._message_templates.get(template_name)
    78→        if template is None:
    79→            raise ConfigurationError(f"Message template not found: {template_name}")
    80→        return template
    81→
    82→    def list_templates(self) -> list:
    83→        """List all available message template names."""
    84→        return list(self._message_templates.keys())
    85→
    86→
    87→# Global configuration instance
    88→_config_instance = None
    89→
    90→
    91→def get_config() -> Config:
    92→    """Get global configuration instance."""
    93→    global _config_instance
    94→    if _config_instance is None:
    95→        _config_instance = Config()
    96→    return _config_instance
    97→
    98→
    99→

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17→
    18→    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19→    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20→    """
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23→        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration.
    24→
    25→        Args:
    26→            config: Config instance (uses global singleton if None)
    27→            db_manager: DatabaseManager instance (uses global singleton if None)
    28→        """
    29→        self.config = config or get_config()
    30→        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    31→
    32→        # Adaptive throttle state
    33→        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, <1.0 = stricter
    34→        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    35→        self._error_timestamps: list = []
    36→        self._pruned: bool = False  # one-time prune flag
    37→
    38→    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    39→        """Report a captcha occurrence to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    40→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    41→        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    42→        # Keep only last 30 minutes of captcha events
    43→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    44→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    45→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    46→
    47→    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    48→        """Recalculate throttle factor based on recent captcha/error frequency.
    49→
    50→        Throttle factor ranges from 1.0 (normal) to 0.3 (heavily throttled).
    51→        - 1 captcha in 30min: reduce to 0.7x (30% fewer operations allowed)
    52→        - 2 captchas: reduce to 0.5x
    53→        - 3+ captchas: reduce to 0.3x
    54→        - Errors contribute at half the weight of captchas
    55→        """
    56→        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    57→        error_count = len(self._error_timestamps)
    58→        combined = captcha_count + error_count * 0.5
    59→
    60→        if combined >= 3:
    61→            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    62→        elif combined >= 2:
    63→            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    64→        elif combined >= 1:
    65→            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    66→        else:
    67→            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    68→
    69→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    70→            logger.warning(
    71→                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    72→                f"(captchas={captcha_count}, errors={error_count} in last 30min)"
    73→            )
    74→
    75→    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    76→        """Prune expired timestamps and recalculate throttle factor."""
    77→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
    78→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    79→        self._error_timestamps = [t for t in self._error_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    80→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    81→
    82→    @property
    83→    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    84→        """Current adaptive throttle factor (1.0 = normal, <1.0 = throttled)."""
    85→        return self._throttle_factor
    86→
    87→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    88→        """
    89→        Get rate limits for an action type, adjusted by adaptive throttle.
    90→
    91→        Args:
    92→            action_type: Type of action ('search', 'view_profile', 'send_message')
    93→
    94→        Returns:
    95→            Tuple of (per_hour_limit, per_day_limit, min_interval_seconds)
    96→        """
    97→        action_map = {
    98→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
    99→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
   100→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
   101→        }
   102→
   103→        if action_type not in action_map:
   104→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
   105→
   106→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
   107→
   108→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
   109→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
   110→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 30)
   111→
   112→        # Apply adaptive throttle: reduce limits and increase interval
   113→        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
   114→        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
   115→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
   116→            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
   117→
   118→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
   119→
   120→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
   121→        """
   122→        Count actions of a type since a given time.
   123→
   124→        Args:
   125→            action_type: Type of action
   126→            since: Count actions since this time
   127→
   128→        Returns:
   129→            Number of actions
   130→        """
   131→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   132→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
   133→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   134→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
   135→            ).scalar()
   136→
   137→            return count or 0
   138→
   139→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
   140→        """
   141→        Get timestamp of last action.
   142→
   143→        Args:
   144→            action_type: Type of action, or None for any action
   145→
   146→        Returns:
   147→            Timestamp of last action, or None if no actions
   148→        """
   149→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   150→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   151→
   152→            if action_type:
   153→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   154→
   155→            last_time = query.scalar()
   156→            return last_time
   157→
   158→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   159→        """
   160→        Check if action is allowed under rate limits.
   161→
   162→        Args:
   163→            action_type: Type of action to check
   164→
   165→        Raises:
   166→            RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit is exceeded
   167→        """
   168→        # Prune stale entries once per session to keep counts accurate
   169→        if not self._pruned:
   170→            self.prune_old_entries()
   171→            self._pruned = True
   172→
   173→        self._refresh_throttle()
   174→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   175→
   176→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   177→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   178→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   179→
   180→        # Check hourly limit
   181→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   182→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   183→            # Calculate wait time until oldest action expires
   184→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   185→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   186→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   187→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   188→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   189→
   190→                if oldest_in_window:
   191→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
   192→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   193→                else:
   194→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   195→
   196→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   197→                action_type=action_type,
   198→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   199→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   200→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   201→            )
   202→
   203→        # Check daily limit
   204→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   205→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   206→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   207→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   208→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   209→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   210→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   211→
   212→                if oldest_in_window:
   213→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
   214→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   215→                else:
   216→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   217→
   218→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   219→                action_type=action_type,
   220→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   221→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   222→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   223→            )
   224→
   225→        # Check minimum interval (per-action-type to avoid cross-type blocking)
   226→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type)
   227→        if last_action:
   228→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   229→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   230→                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
   231→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   232→                    action_type='any',
   233→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   234→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   235→                )
   236→
   237→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   238→
   239→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   240→        """
   241→        Log an action to the database.
   242→
   243→        Args:
   244→            action_type: Type of action
   245→            details: Optional action details
   246→        """
   247→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   248→            action_log = ActionLog(
   249→                action_type=action_type,
   250→                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
   251→                details=details or {}
   252→            )
   253→            session.add(action_log)
   254→
   255→        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   256→
   257→    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
   258→        """Remove ActionLog entries older than the retention window.
   259→
   260→        This prevents stale counts from accumulating across sessions and
   261→        keeps the ActionLog table from growing indefinitely.
   262→
   263→        Args:
   264→            retention_hours: Delete entries older than this many hours (default 48)
   265→
   266→        Returns:
   267→            Number of entries deleted
   268→        """
   269→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
   270→        try:
   271→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   272→                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
   273→                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
   274→                ).delete()
   275→                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
   276→                return count
   277→        except Exception as e:
   278→            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
   279→            return 0
   280→
   281→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   282→        """
   283→        Get current quota status for an action type.
   284→
   285→        Args:
   286→            action_type: Type of action
   287→
   288→        Returns:
   289→            Dictionary with quota information
   290→        """
   291→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   292→
   293→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   294→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   295→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   296→
   297→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   298→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   299→
   300→        return {
   301→            'action_type': action_type,
   302→            'hourly': {
   303→                'used': hourly_used,
   304→                'limit': per_hour,
   305→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   306→            },
   307→            'daily': {
   308→                'used': daily_used,
   309→                'limit': per_day,
   310→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   311→            }
   312→        }
   313→
   314→
   315→async def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str, max_retries: int = 60) -> None:
   316→    """
   317→    Check rate limit, waiting through any violations until the action is allowed.
   318→
   319→    Blocks until the rate limit clears. Raises RateLimitExceededError if
   320→    still blocked after max_retries attempts (prevents infinite loops from
   321→    persistent failures such as corrupt DB data).
   322→
   323→    Args:
   324→        action_type: Type of action to check
   325→        max_retries: Maximum wait-retry cycles before giving up
   326→    """
   327→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   328→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
   329→        try:
   330→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   331→            return
   332→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   333→            if attempt >= max_retries - 1:
   334→                raise
   335→            wait = e.wait_time + 1  # +1s buffer
   336→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries})...")
   337→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   338→
   339→
   340→# Global rate limiter instance
   341→_rate_limiter = None
   342→
   343→
   344→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   345→    """Get global rate limiter instance."""
   346→    global _rate_limiter
   347→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   348→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   349→    return _rate_limiter
   350→

1→"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation.
     2→
     3→Uses log-normal distribution instead of uniform for more realistic timing.
     4→Includes fatigue simulation (delays increase over session duration) and
     5→random "distraction" pauses to mimic real human behavior.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import datetime as dt_module
     9→import math
    10→import random
    11→import asyncio
    12→import time
    13→from loguru import logger
    14→from src.utils.config import get_config
    15→
    16→
    17→# Session start time for fatigue calculation
    18→_session_start: float = time.monotonic()
    19→
    20→# Action counter for distraction triggers
    21→_action_count: int = 0
    22→
    23→
    24→def reset_session():
    25→    """Reset fatigue/distraction tracking for a new command invocation."""
    26→    global _session_start, _action_count
    27→    _session_start = time.monotonic()
    28→    _action_count = 0
    29→
    30→
    31→def _lognormal_delay(min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    32→    """Generate a log-normal distributed delay between min and max.
    33→
    34→    Log-normal produces a right-skewed distribution: most delays are
    35→    near the low end, with occasional longer pauses — matching human behavior.
    36→    """
    37→    # Map min/max to log-normal mu/sigma
    38→    mid = (min_val + max_val) / 2
    39→    mu = math.log(mid)
    40→    sigma = 0.4  # Controls spread; 0.4 gives moderate variance
    41→    delay = random.lognormvariate(mu, sigma)
    42→    return max(min_val, min(delay, max_val * 1.5))
    43→
    44→
    45→def _fatigue_multiplier() -> float:
    46→    """Calculate fatigue multiplier based on session duration.
    47→
    48→    Humans slow down over time. After 30 minutes, delays increase by ~20%.
    49→    After 1 hour, by ~40%. Caps at 1.6x.
    50→    """
    51→    elapsed_minutes = (time.monotonic() - _session_start) / 60.0
    52→    if elapsed_minutes < 10:
    53→        return 1.0
    54→    # Gradual ramp: 1.0 at 10min → ~1.6 at 90min
    55→    factor = 1.0 + 0.006 * min(elapsed_minutes - 10, 100)
    56→    return min(factor, 1.6)
    57→
    58→
    59→def _maybe_distraction_pause() -> float:
    60→    """Occasionally return a long "distraction" pause.
    61→
    62→    About 3% chance of a 5-30 second pause (simulating checking phone,
    63→    reading something, etc.). Returns 0 most of the time.
    64→    """
    65→    global _action_count
    66→    _action_count += 1
    67→
    68→    # Don't trigger on first few actions
    69→    if _action_count < 5:
    70→        return 0.0
    71→
    72→    if random.random() < 0.03:
    73→        pause = random.uniform(5.0, 30.0)
    74→        logger.debug(f"Simulating distraction pause: {pause:.1f}s")
    75→        return pause
    76→    return 0.0
    77→
    78→
    79→def _time_of_day_multiplier() -> float:
    80→    """Adjust delays based on time of day.
    81→
    82→    Late night (23:00-07:00): slower (1.3x) — fewer people browse late.
    83→    Peak hours (10:00-12:00, 14:00-17:00): normal (1.0x).
    84→    Other times: slightly slower (1.1x).
    85→    """
    86→    hour = dt_module.datetime.now().hour
    87→
    88→    if 23 <= hour or hour < 7:
    89→        return 1.3
    90→    elif (10 <= hour <= 12) or (14 <= hour <= 17):
    91→        return 1.0
    92→    else:
    93→        return 1.1
    94→
    95→
    96→def get_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action') -> float:
    97→    """
    98→    Get a random delay based on configured ranges.
    99→
   100→    Uses log-normal distribution with fatigue, distraction, and
   101→    time-of-day adjustments for realistic human behavior.
   102→
   103→    Args:
   104→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll', 'message'
   105→
   106→    Returns:
   107→        Random delay in seconds
   108→    """
   109→    config = get_config()
   110→
   111→    delay_ranges = {
   112→        'page_load': (
   113→            config.get('delays.page_load_min', 2),
   114→            config.get('delays.page_load_max', 5)
   115→        ),
   116→        'action': (
   117→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
   118→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
   119→        ),
   120→        'typing': (
   121→            config.get('delays.typing_min', 0.05),
   122→            config.get('delays.typing_max', 0.15)
   123→        ),
   124→        'scroll': (
   125→            config.get('delays.scroll_min', 0.5),
   126→            config.get('delays.scroll_max', 1.5)
   127→        ),
   128→        'message': (
   129→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
   130→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
   131→        ),
   132→    }
   133→
   134→    min_delay, max_delay = delay_ranges.get(delay_type, (0.5, 2.0))
   135→
   136→    # Use log-normal instead of uniform
   137→    base_delay = _lognormal_delay(min_delay, max_delay)
   138→
   139→    # Apply fatigue (typing is exempt — fatigue doesn't affect keystroke speed much)
   140→    if delay_type != 'typing':
   141→        base_delay *= _fatigue_multiplier()
   142→        base_delay *= _time_of_day_multiplier()
   143→
   144→    # Add possible distraction pause for non-typing delays
   145→    if delay_type in ('action', 'page_load', 'message'):
   146→        base_delay += _maybe_distraction_pause()
   147→
   148→    return base_delay
   149→
   150→
   151→async def async_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
   152→    """
   153→    Asynchronous version of get_random_delay.
   154→
   155→    Args:
   156→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll', 'message'
   157→    """
   158→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
   159→    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   160→
   161→
   162→
   163→def get_retry_delay(attempt: int, base_delay: float = 1.0, max_delay: float = 60.0) -> float:
   164→    """
   165→    Calculate exponential backoff delay for retries.
   166→
   167→    Args:
   168→        attempt: Current retry attempt number (0-indexed)
   169→        base_delay: Base delay in seconds
   170→        max_delay: Maximum delay cap
   171→
   172→    Returns:
   173→        Delay in seconds with jitter
   174→    """
   175→    # Exponential backoff: base * 2^attempt
   176→    delay = min(base_delay * (2 ** attempt), max_delay)
   177→
   178→    # Add jitter (±25%)
   179→    jitter = delay * random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25)
   180→    return max(0, delay + jitter)
   181→

1→"""Pipeline checkpoint manager for resume capability.
     2→
     3→Saves pipeline progress to a JSON file so that interrupted pipelines
     4→can be resumed from where they left off.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import json
     8→import os
     9→import tempfile
    10→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set
    13→
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→
    17→def _get_checkpoint_dir() -> Path:
    18→    """Resolve checkpoint directory from config or use default."""
    19→    try:
    20→        from src.utils.config import get_config
    21→        config = get_config()
    22→        return Path(config.get('data.checkpoint_dir', 'data/.checkpoints'))
    23→    except Exception:
    24→        return Path("data/.checkpoints")
    25→
    26→
    27→class CheckpointManager:
    28→    """Manages pipeline checkpoint save/load for resume support."""
    29→
    30→    def __init__(self, checkpoint_id: Optional[str] = None):
    31→        checkpoint_dir = _get_checkpoint_dir()
    32→        checkpoint_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    33→
    34→        if checkpoint_id:
    35→            self.checkpoint_path = checkpoint_dir / f"{checkpoint_id}.json"
    36→        else:
    37→            # Generate ID from timestamp
    38→            ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    39→            self.checkpoint_path = checkpoint_dir / f"pipeline_{ts}.json"
    40→
    41→        self._data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
    42→
    43→    def save(
    44→        self,
    45→        keywords: tuple,
    46→        template: str,
    47→        variables: dict,
    48→        company: tuple,
    49→        location: Optional[str],
    50→        max_pages: int,
    51→        batch_pages: int,
    52→        skip_messaged: bool,
    53→        interactive: bool,
    54→        completed_pages: int,
    55→        sent_ids: List[str],
    56→        seen_ids: List[str],
    57→        total_results: Optional[dict] = None,
    58→        mode: str = "search_center",
    59→        dry_run: bool = False,
    60→    ) -> None:
    61→        """Save current pipeline state to checkpoint file."""
    62→        self._data = {
    63→            "created_at": self._data.get("created_at", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),
    64→            "updated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    65→            "params": {
    66→                "keywords": list(keywords),
    67→                "template": template,
    68→                "variables": variables,
    69→                "company": list(company) if company else [],
    70→                "location": location,
    71→                "max_pages": max_pages,
    72→                "batch_pages": batch_pages,
    73→                "skip_messaged": skip_messaged,
    74→                "interactive": interactive,
    75→                "mode": mode,
    76→                "dry_run": dry_run,
    77→            },
    78→            "progress": {
    79→                "completed_pages": completed_pages,
    80→                "sent_ids": sent_ids,
    81→                "seen_ids": seen_ids,
    82→                "total_results": total_results or {},
    83→            },
    84→        }
    85→
    86→        try:
    87→            tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(
    88→                dir=self.checkpoint_path.parent, suffix=".tmp"
    89→            )
    90→            try:
    91→                with os.fdopen(tmp_fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    92→                    json.dump(self._data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    93→                os.replace(tmp_path, self.checkpoint_path)
    94→            except BaseException:
    95→                try:
    96→                    os.unlink(tmp_path)
    97→                except OSError:
    98→                    pass
    99→                raise
   100→            logger.debug(f"Checkpoint saved: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   101→        except Exception as e:
   102→            logger.error(f"Failed to save checkpoint: {e}")
   103→            raise
   104→
   105→    @classmethod
   106→    def load_latest(cls) -> Optional["CheckpointManager"]:
   107→        """Load the most recent checkpoint file.
   108→
   109→        Returns:
   110→            CheckpointManager with loaded data, or None if no checkpoint exists.
   111→        """
   112→        checkpoint_dir = _get_checkpoint_dir()
   113→        checkpoint_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   114→        files = sorted(checkpoint_dir.glob("pipeline_*.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
   115→
   116→        if not files:
   117→            return None
   118→
   119→        mgr = cls(checkpoint_id="__loading__")
   120→        mgr.checkpoint_path = files[0]
   121→
   122→        try:
   123→            with open(files[0], "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   124→                mgr._data = json.load(f)
   125→            logger.info(f"Loaded checkpoint: {files[0]}")
   126→            return mgr
   127→        except Exception as e:
   128→            logger.error(f"Failed to load checkpoint: {e}")
   129→            return None
   130→
   131→    @property
   132→    def params(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   133→        return self._data.get("params", {})
   134→
   135→    @property
   136→    def progress(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   137→        return self._data.get("progress", {})
   138→
   139→    @property
   140→    def completed_pages(self) -> int:
   141→        return self.progress.get("completed_pages", 0)
   142→
   143→    @property
   144→    def sent_ids(self) -> List[str]:
   145→        return list(self.progress.get("sent_ids", []))
   146→
   147→    @property
   148→    def seen_ids(self) -> Set[str]:
   149→        return set(self.progress.get("seen_ids", []))
   150→
   151→    @property
   152→    def total_results(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   153→        return self.progress.get("total_results", {})
   154→
   155→    @property
   156→    def summary(self) -> str:
   157→        """Human-readable summary of checkpoint state."""
   158→        p = self.params
   159→        pr = self.progress
   160→        created = self._data.get("created_at", "unknown")
   161→        updated = self._data.get("updated_at", "unknown")
   162→        mode = p.get("mode", "search_center")
   163→        return (
   164→            f"Keywords: {p.get('keywords', [])}\n"
   165→            f"Template: {p.get('template', '')}\n"
   166→            f"Mode: {mode}\n"
   167→            f"Pages completed: {pr.get('completed_pages', 0)} / {p.get('max_pages', '?')}\n"
   168→            f"Candidates seen: {len(pr.get('seen_ids', []))}\n"
   169→            f"Messages sent: {len(pr.get('sent_ids', []))}\n"
   170→            f"Created: {created}\n"
   171→            f"Last updated: {updated}"
   172→        )
   173→
   174→    def delete(self) -> None:
   175→        """Remove checkpoint file after successful completion."""
   176→        try:
   177→            if self.checkpoint_path.exists():
   178→                self.checkpoint_path.unlink()
   179→                logger.debug(f"Checkpoint deleted: {self.checkpoint_path}")
   180→        except Exception as e:
   181→            logger.error(f"Failed to delete checkpoint: {e}")
   182→

1→"""Candidate scoring for priority-based messaging.
     2→
     3→Scores candidates based on title seniority, company tier, and keyword
     4→relevance so that the best-fit candidates get contacted first.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import re
     8→from typing import Dict, List, Optional
     9→
    10→from loguru import logger
    11→
    12→
    13→# Seniority keywords and their score weights
    14→SENIORITY_SCORES: Dict[str, int] = {
    15→    # C-level
    16→    "CTO": 50, "CEO": 40, "COO": 35, "VP": 40,
    17→    "首席": 45, "总裁": 40, "副总裁": 40,
    18→    # Director
    19→    "总监": 35, "技术总监": 40,
    20→    # Senior management
    21→    "技术负责人": 35, "负责人": 30, "Tech Lead": 35,
    22→    # Senior individual contributors
    23→    "架构师": 35, "专家": 30, "staff": 30, "Staff": 30,
    24→    "principal": 35, "Principal": 35,
    25→    "资深": 25, "高级": 20, "Senior": 20, "senior": 20,
    26→    # Mid-level
    27→    "经理": 15, "主管": 15, "lead": 15, "Lead": 15,
    28→    "中级": 10,
    29→    # Junior
    30→    "初级": 5, "Junior": 5, "junior": 5,
    31→    "助理": 3, "实习": 1,
    32→}
    33→
    34→# Well-known tech companies (tier 1)
    35→TIER1_COMPANIES = {
    36→    "字节跳动", "ByteDance", "腾讯", "Tencent", "阿里巴巴", "阿里", "Alibaba",
    37→    "百度", "Baidu", "美团", "Meituan", "京东", "JD", "拼多多", "PDD",
    38→    "华为", "Huawei", "小米", "Xiaomi", "网易", "NetEase", "快手", "Kwai",
    39→    "蚂蚁集团", "蚂蚁金服", "滴滴", "DiDi",
    40→    "Google", "谷歌", "Microsoft", "微软", "Apple", "苹果",
    41→    "Amazon", "亚马逊", "Meta", "Facebook",
    42→    "Netflix", "Uber", "OpenAI", "Anthropic",
    43→}
    44→
    45→# Good tech companies (tier 2)
    46→TIER2_COMPANIES = {
    47→    "携程", "Trip.com", "B站", "bilibili", "哔哩哔哩",
    48→    "知乎", "Zhihu", "小红书", "得物", "SHEIN",
    49→    "商汤", "旷视", "依图", "寒武纪", "地平线",
    50→    "理想汽车", "蔚来", "小鹏", "大疆", "DJI",
    51→    "米哈游", "miHoYo", "莉莉丝", "叠纸",
    52→    "360", "新浪", "微博", "搜狐", "爱奇艺",
    53→    "OPPO", "vivo", "一加", "联想", "Lenovo",
    54→    "SAP", "Oracle", "IBM", "Adobe", "Salesforce",
    55→    "Stripe", "Airbnb", "Spotify", "Twitter", "X",
    56→}
    57→
    58→
    59→def score_candidate(
    60→    name: str,
    61→    title: str,
    62→    company: str,
    63→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    64→) -> int:
    65→    """
    66→    Score a candidate based on title, company, and keyword relevance.
    67→
    68→    Higher score = higher priority for messaging.
    69→
    70→    Scoring breakdown:
    71→    - Title seniority: 0-50 points
    72→    - Company tier: 0-30 points
    73→    - Keyword match: 0-20 points
    74→
    75→    Args:
    76→        name: Candidate name
    77→        title: Job title
    78→        company: Company name
    79→        search_keywords: Original search keywords for relevance scoring
    80→
    81→    Returns:
    82→        Integer score (higher = better fit)
    83→    """
    84→    score = 0
    85→
    86→    # 1. Title seniority scoring
    87→    title_lower = title.lower() if title else ""
    88→    best_seniority = 0
    89→    for keyword, points in SENIORITY_SCORES.items():
    90→        if keyword.lower() in title_lower or keyword in title:
    91→            best_seniority = max(best_seniority, points)
    92→    score += best_seniority
    93→
    94→    # 2. Company tier scoring
    95→    # Require minimum 2 characters on both sides to avoid false positives
    96→    # from short names like "X", "JD" matching substrings of unrelated companies
    97→    if company:
    98→        company_lower = company.lower()
    99→        company_matched = False
   100→        for t1 in TIER1_COMPANIES:
   101→            t1_lower = t1.lower()
   102→            if len(t1_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   103→                if t1_lower == company_lower or (len(t1_lower) >= 3 and t1_lower in company_lower) or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t1_lower):
   104→                    score += 30
   105→                    company_matched = True
   106→                    break
   107→        if not company_matched:
   108→            for t2 in TIER2_COMPANIES:
   109→                t2_lower = t2.lower()
   110→                if len(t2_lower) >= 2 and len(company_lower) >= 2:
   111→                    if t2_lower == company_lower or (len(t2_lower) >= 3 and t2_lower in company_lower) or (len(company_lower) >= 3 and company_lower in t2_lower):
   112→                        score += 20
   113→                        break
   114→
   115→    # 3. Keyword relevance (if search keywords provided), capped at 20 points
   116→    if search_keywords and title:
   117→        keyword_score = 0
   118→        for kw in search_keywords:
   119→            kw_lower = kw.lower()
   120→            if kw_lower in title_lower:
   121→                keyword_score += 10
   122→            elif kw_lower in (company or "").lower():
   123→                keyword_score += 5
   124→        score += min(keyword_score, 20)
   125→
   126→    # 4. Bonus: R&D-specific titles
   127→    rd_bonus_patterns = [
   128→        r"工程师", r"开发", r"架构", r"算法", r"研发",
   129→        r"engineer", r"developer", r"architect",
   130→    ]
   131→    for pattern in rd_bonus_patterns:
   132→        if re.search(pattern, title, re.IGNORECASE):
   133→            score += 5
   134→            break
   135→
   136→    return score
   137→
   138→
   139→def sort_candidates_by_score(
   140→    candidates: list,
   141→    search_keywords: Optional[List[str]] = None,
   142→) -> list:
   143→    """
   144→    Sort a list of candidate dicts or objects by score (descending).
   145→
   146→    Works with both CandidateBasic dataclass instances and dicts.
   147→
   148→    Args:
   149→        candidates: List of candidates (dicts or objects with name/title/company)
   150→        search_keywords: Original search keywords for relevance scoring
   151→
   152→    Returns:
   153→        Sorted list (highest score first), each item gets a '_score' attribute/key
   154→    """
   155→    scored = []
   156→    for c in candidates:
   157→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   158→            name = c.get("name", "")
   159→            title = c.get("title", "")
   160→            company = c.get("company", "")
   161→        else:
   162→            name = getattr(c, "name", "")
   163→            title = getattr(c, "title", "")
   164→            company = getattr(c, "company", "")
   165→
   166→        s = score_candidate(name, title, company, search_keywords)
   167→
   168→        if isinstance(c, dict):
   169→            c["_score"] = s
   170→        else:
   171→            c._score = s
   172→
   173→        scored.append((s, c))
   174→
   175→    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
   176→
   177→    result = [item[1] for item in scored]
   178→
   179→    if result:
   180→        top = scored[0][0]
   181→        bottom = scored[-1][0]
   182→        logger.info(f"Candidates scored: top={top}, bottom={bottom}, count={len(result)}")
   183→
   184→    return result
   185→

1→"""Input validation utilities."""
     2→
     3→import re
     4→from typing import Dict, Any
     5→
     6→from src.exceptions.errors import ValidationError
     7→
     8→
     9→def validate_maimai_id(maimai_id: str) -> str:
    10→    """
    11→    Validate Maimai user ID format.
    12→
    13→    Args:
    14→        maimai_id: Maimai user ID to validate
    15→
    16→    Returns:
    17→        Validated and cleaned ID
    18→
    19→    Raises:
    20→        ValidationError: If ID format is invalid
    21→    """
    22→    if not maimai_id:
    23→        raise ValidationError("Maimai ID cannot be empty")
    24→
    25→    # Remove whitespace
    26→    maimai_id = str(maimai_id).strip()
    27→
    28→    # Check if it's alphanumeric
    29→    if not re.match(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$', maimai_id):
    30→        raise ValidationError(f"Invalid Maimai ID format: {maimai_id}")
    31→
    32→    return maimai_id
    33→
    34→
    35→def validate_keywords(keywords: str) -> str:
    36→    """
    37→    Validate search keywords.
    38→
    39→    Args:
    40→        keywords: Search keywords
    41→
    42→    Returns:
    43→        Validated keywords
    44→
    45→    Raises:
    46→        ValidationError: If keywords are invalid
    47→    """
    48→    if not keywords or not keywords.strip():
    49→        raise ValidationError("Search keywords cannot be empty")
    50→
    51→    keywords = keywords.strip()
    52→
    53→    # Check length
    54→    if len(keywords) < 2:
    55→        raise ValidationError("Search keywords must be at least 2 characters")
    56→
    57→    if len(keywords) > 200:
    58→        raise ValidationError("Search keywords cannot exceed 200 characters")
    59→
    60→    return keywords
    61→
    62→
    63→def validate_template_variables(template: Dict[str, Any], variables: Dict[str, str]) -> None:
    64→    """
    65→    Validate that all required template variables are provided.
    66→
    67→    Args:
    68→        template: Template dictionary with 'variables' key
    69→        variables: Provided variables dictionary
    70→
    71→    Raises:
    72→        ValidationError: If required variables are missing
    73→    """
    74→    required_vars = template.get('variables', [])
    75→    provided_vars = set(variables.keys())
    76→    required_set = set(required_vars)
    77→
    78→    missing_vars = required_set - provided_vars
    79→
    80→    if missing_vars:
    81→        raise ValidationError(
    82→            f"Missing required template variables: {', '.join(missing_vars)}"
    83→        )
    84→
    85→

1→"""Logging configuration using Loguru."""
     2→
     3→import sys
     4→from pathlib import Path
     5→
     6→from loguru import logger
     7→
     8→from src.utils.config import get_config
     9→
    10→
    11→def setup_logger():
    12→    """Configure Loguru logger with settings from config."""
    13→    config = get_config()
    14→
    15→    # Remove default handler
    16→    logger.remove()
    17→
    18→    # Get logging settings
    19→    log_level = config.get('logging.level', 'INFO')
    20→    log_format = config.get('logging.format')
    21→    rotation = config.get('logging.rotation', '10 MB')
    22→    retention = config.get('logging.retention', '30 days')
    23→    log_dir = Path(config.get('data.log_dir', 'data/logs'))
    24→
    25→    # Create log directory if it doesn't exist
    26→    log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    27→
    28→    # Add console handler with colors
    29→    logger.add(
    30→        sys.stdout,
    31→        format=log_format,
    32→        level=log_level,
    33→        colorize=True,
    34→    )
    35→
    36→    # Add file handler with rotation
    37→    logger.add(
    38→        log_dir / "maimai_assistant_{time:YYYY-MM-DD}.log",
    39→        format=log_format,
    40→        level=log_level,
    41→        rotation=rotation,
    42→        retention=retention,
    43→        encoding='utf-8',
    44→    )
    45→
    46→    logger.info(f"Logger initialized with level: {log_level}")
    47→
    48→
    49→

1→"""Retry utilities for navigation and network operations."""
     2→
     3→from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential, retry_if_exception_type
     4→from patchright.async_api import Page, Error as PlaywrightError
     5→from loguru import logger
     6→
     7→
     8→@retry(
     9→    stop=stop_after_attempt(3),
    10→    wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=2, max=10),
    11→    retry=retry_if_exception_type((PlaywrightError, TimeoutError, ConnectionError)),
    12→    before_sleep=lambda retry_state: logger.warning(
    13→        f"Navigation failed (attempt {retry_state.attempt_number}), retrying..."
    14→    ),
    15→    reraise=True,
    16→)
    17→async def retry_navigation(page: Page, url: str, **kwargs) -> None:
    18→    """Navigate to a URL with automatic retry on failure.
    19→
    20→    Args:
    21→        page: Playwright page instance
    22→        url: URL to navigate to
    23→        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to page.goto()
    24→    """
    25→    await page.goto(url, **kwargs)
    26→

1→"""Shared captcha detection utility.
     2→
     3→Detects captcha elements on page and pauses for manual resolution.
     4→Used by auth and search modules.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import asyncio
     8→import sys
     9→
    10→from patchright.async_api import Page
    11→from loguru import logger
    12→from rich.console import Console
    13→
    14→console = Console()
    15→
    16→CAPTCHA_SELECTORS = [
    17→    '[class*="captcha"]',
    18→    '[class*="verify"]',
    19→    '[id*="captcha"]',
    20→    '[class*="slider"]',
    21→    '[class*="geetest"]',
    22→    'iframe[src*="captcha"]',
    23→]
    24→
    25→
    26→async def check_captcha(page: Page, report_to_rate_limiter: bool = False) -> None:
    27→    """Detect captcha elements on page and pause for manual resolution.
    28→
    29→    Args:
    30→        page: Playwright page instance
    31→        report_to_rate_limiter: If True, report captcha to adaptive rate limiter
    32→    """
    33→    for selector in CAPTCHA_SELECTORS:
    34→        try:
    35→            elem = await page.query_selector(selector)
    36→            if elem and await elem.is_visible():
    37→                if report_to_rate_limiter:
    38→                    from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter
    39→                    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
    40→                    rate_limiter.report_captcha()
    41→
    42→                console.print("[bold red]Captcha detected! Please solve it in the browser window.[/bold red]")
    43→                console.print("[dim]Press Enter here after solving the captcha...[/dim]")
    44→                loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    45→                await loop.run_in_executor(None, sys.stdin.readline)
    46→                await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    47→                return
    48→        except Exception:
    49→            continue
    50→

1→"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""
     2→
     3→from contextlib import contextmanager

     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from sqlalchemy import (
     9→    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    10→    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint
    11→)
    12→from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, relationship
    13→from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config
    17→
    18→Base = declarative_base()
    19→
    20→
    21→def _utcnow():
    22→    """Return timezone-aware UTC now (replaces deprecated datetime.utcnow)."""
    23→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    24→
    25→
    26→class Candidate(Base):
    27→    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""
    28→
    29→    __tablename__ = 'candidates'
    30→
    31→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    32→    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    33→    name = Column(String(100))
    34→    title = Column(String(200))
    35→    company = Column(String(200))
    36→    location = Column(String(100))
    37→    profile_url = Column(Text)
    38→
    39→    # JSON fields for structured data
    40→    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    41→    education = Column(JSON)
    42→    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    43→    skills = Column(JSON)
    44→
    45→    # Metadata
    46→    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    47→    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, onupdate=_utcnow)
    48→    source_search = Column(Text)  # Search keywords that found this candidate
    49→
    50→    # Relationships
    51→    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")
    52→
    53→    def __repr__(self):
    54→        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"
    55→
    56→
    57→class Message(Base):
    58→    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""
    59→
    60→    __tablename__ = 'messages'
    61→
    62→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    63→    candidate_maimai_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.maimai_id'), index=True)
    64→    template_name = Column(String(100))
    65→    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    66→    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    67→    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    68→    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    69→
    70→    # Relationships
    71→    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")
    72→
    73→    def __repr__(self):
    74→        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"
    75→
    76→
    77→class SearchHistory(Base):
    78→    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""
    79→
    80→    __tablename__ = 'search_history'
    81→
    82→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    83→    criteria = Column(JSON)  # Search parameters as JSON
    84→    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    85→    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, index=True)
    86→
    87→    def __repr__(self):
    88→        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"
    89→
    90→
    91→class ActionLog(Base):
    92→    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""
    93→
    94→    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'
    95→
    96→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    97→    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)  # 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
    98→    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    99→    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details
   100→
   101→    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries
   102→    __table_args__ = (
   103→        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
   104→    )
   105→
   106→    def __repr__(self):
   107→        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"
   108→
   109→
   110→class DatabaseManager:
   111→    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""
   112→
   113→    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
   114→        """Initialize database manager."""
   115→        if db_path is None:
   116→            config = get_config()
   117→            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')
   118→
   119→        # Ensure data directory exists
   120→        db_file = Path(db_path)
   121→        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   122→
   123→        # Create engine and session
   124→        # StaticPool uses a single connection, avoiding SQLite "database is locked" errors
   125→        self.db_path = db_path
   126→        self.engine = create_engine(
   127→            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
   128→            echo=False,
   129→            poolclass=StaticPool,
   130→            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
   131→        )
   132→        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
   133→
   134→        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
   135→        self.init_db()
   136→
   137→        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")
   138→
   139→    def init_db(self):
   140→        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
   141→        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
   142→        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")
   143→
   144→        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
   145→        run_migrations(self.engine)
   146→
   147→    @contextmanager
   148→    def session_scope(self):
   149→        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
   150→        session = self.Session()
   151→        try:
   152→            yield session
   153→            session.commit()
   154→        except Exception:
   155→            session.rollback()
   156→            raise
   157→        finally:
   158→            session.close()
   159→
   160→    def drop_all(self):
   161→        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
   162→        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
   163→        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")
   164→
   165→
   166→# Global database manager instance
   167→_db_manager = None
   168→
   169→
   170→def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
   171→    """Get global database manager instance."""
   172→    global _db_manager
   173→    if _db_manager is None:
   174→        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
   175→    return _db_manager
   176→

1→"""Lightweight database migration system.
     2→
     3→Tracks schema versions in a dedicated table and applies incremental
     4→migrations on startup.  Each migration is a plain function that
     5→receives a SQLAlchemy connection.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from typing import Callable, List, Tuple
     9→
    10→from sqlalchemy import text, inspect
    11→from loguru import logger
    12→
    13→
    14→# Migration registry: list of (version, description, migration_fn)
    15→# Append new migrations at the end; never reorder or remove existing entries.
    16→_migrations: List[Tuple[int, str, Callable]] = []
    17→
    18→
    19→def migration(version: int, description: str):
    20→    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    21→    def decorator(fn: Callable):
    22→        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
    23→        return fn
    24→    return decorator
    25→
    26→
    27→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→#  Built-in migrations
    29→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→
    31→@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
    32→def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    33→    """Ensure core tables exist (safe for existing databases)."""
    34→    # SQLAlchemy create_all is called before migrations, so tables should
    35→    # already exist.  This migration just marks the baseline version.
    36→    pass
    37→
    38→
    39→@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
    40→def _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    41→    inspector = inspect(conn)
    42→    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    43→    if "retry_count" not in columns:
    44→        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))
    45→
    46→
    47→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→#  Migration runner
    49→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→
    51→def _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    52→    """Create the schema_version table if it doesn't exist."""
    53→    conn.execute(text("""
    54→        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
    55→            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    56→            description TEXT NOT NULL,
    57→            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    58→        )
    59→    """))
    60→    conn.commit()
    61→
    62→
    63→def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    64→    """Return highest applied migration version, or 0 if none."""
    65→    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    66→    row = result.fetchone()
    67→    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0
    68→
    69→
    70→def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    71→    """Apply all pending migrations against *engine*.
    72→
    73→    Called automatically by DatabaseManager.init_db().
    74→    """
    75→    with engine.connect() as conn:
    76→        _ensure_version_table(conn)
    77→        current = _current_version(conn)
    78→
    79→        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
    80→        if not pending:
    81→            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
    82→            return
    83→
    84→        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
    85→            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
    86→            fn(conn)
    87→            conn.execute(
    88→                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
    89→                {"v": version, "d": description},
    90→            )
    91→            conn.commit()
    92→
    93→        final = _current_version(conn)
    94→        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")
    95→

1→"""Pipeline service for search-and-message orchestration.
     2→
     3→Extracted from CLI layer to separate business logic from argument parsing.
     4→Supports dual-path: search_center (Path A) and enterprise (Path B).
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from datetime import datetime
     8→from enum import Enum
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple
    11→
    12→from rich.console import Console
    13→from rich.table import Table

    21→class SearchMode(str, Enum):
    22→    """Search mode for pipeline dispatch."""
    23→    SEARCH_CENTER = "search_center"
    24→    ENTERPRISE = "enterprise"
    25→
    26→
    27→def _candidates_to_dicts(candidates: List[CandidateBasic]) -> List[dict]:
    28→    """Convert CandidateBasic objects to dicts for message batch functions."""
    29→    return [
    30→        {
    31→            'maimai_id': c.maimai_id,
    32→            'name': c.name,
    33→            'company': c.company,
    34→            'title': c.title,
    35→            'profile_url': c.profile_url,
    36→        }
    37→        for c in candidates
    38→    ]
    39→
    40→
    41→async def search_all_keywords(
    42→    browser: BrowserManager,
    43→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
    44→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
    45→    location: Optional[str],
    46→    max_pages: int,
    47→    start_page: int = 1,
    48→) -> Tuple[List[CandidateBasic], Set[str]]:
    49→    """Iterate keywords, deduplicate, return combined results.
    50→
    51→    Args:
    52→        browser: BrowserManager instance
    53→        keywords: Keyword strings to search
    54→        company: Company filter strings
    55→        location: Location filter or None
    56→        max_pages: Pages per keyword
    57→        start_page: First page number
    58→
    59→    Returns:
    60→        (deduplicated candidates list, set of seen IDs)
    61→    """
    62→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
    63→    seen_ids: Set[str] = set()
    64→
    65→    for idx, kw in enumerate(keywords):
    66→        console.print(f"\n[bold blue][{idx+1}/{len(keywords)}] Searching: {kw}[/bold blue]")
    67→
    68→        candidates = await search_candidates(
    69→            keywords=kw,
    70→            company=company,
    71→            location=location,
    72→            pages=max_pages,
    73→            start_page=start_page,
    74→            browser=browser,
    75→        )
    76→
    77→        new_count = 0
    78→        for c in candidates:
    79→            if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids:
    80→                seen_ids.add(c.maimai_id)
    81→                all_candidates.append(c)
    82→                new_count += 1
    83→
    84→        console.print(f"  Found {len(candidates)} candidates, {new_count} new (deduplicated)")
    85→
    86→    return all_candidates, seen_ids
    87→
    88→
    89→async def search_all_keywords_enterprise(
    90→    browser: BrowserManager,
    91→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
    92→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
    93→    max_pages: int,
    94→    keep_last_tab_open: bool = False,
    95→) -> Tuple[List[CandidateBasic], Set[str]]:
    96→    """Iterate keywords via enterprise path, deduplicate, return combined results.
    97→
    98→    Args:
    99→        browser: BrowserManager instance
   100→        keywords: Keyword strings to search
   101→        company: Company filter strings
   102→        max_pages: Number of scroll-pages to extract per keyword
   103→        keep_last_tab_open: Keep the last enterprise tab open for inline messaging
   104→
   105→    Returns:

   107→    """
   108→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   109→    seen_ids: Set[str] = set()
   110→
   111→    for idx, kw in enumerate(keywords):

   113→
   114→        is_last = (idx == len(keywords) - 1)
   115→        candidates = await search_candidates_enterprise(

   123→        new_count = 0

   129→
   130→        console.print(f"  Found {len(candidates)} candidates, {new_count} new (deduplicated)")
   131→
   132→    return all_candidates, seen_ids

   209→def generate_pipeline_report(

   225→    Returns:

   228→    from openpyxl import Workbook
   229→    from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment, Border, Side
   230→    from loguru import logger

   232→    from src.utils.config import get_config
   233→    timestamp = datetime.now()
   234→    config = get_config()
   235→    report_dir = Path(config.get('data.report_dir', 'data/reports'))
   236→    report_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   237→    report_path = report_dir / f"pipeline_{timestamp.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.xlsx"
   238→
   239→    wb = Workbook()
   240→
   241→    # --- Sheet 1: Overview ---
   242→    ws_overview = wb.active
   243→    ws_overview.title = "概览"
   244→
   245→    header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
   246→    header_fill = PatternFill(start_color="4472C4", end_color="4472C4", fill_type="solid")
   247→    border = Border(
   248→        left=Side(style="thin"), right=Side(style="thin"),
   249→        top=Side(style="thin"), bottom=Side(style="thin"),
   250→    )
   251→
   252→    overview_rows = [
   253→        ("执行时间", timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")),
   254→        ("搜索关键词", ", ".join(keywords)),
   255→        ("消息模板", template),
   256→        ("搜索模式", mode.value),
   257→        ("搜索结果数", results["total"]),
   258→        ("发送总数", results["sent"] + results["failed"]),
   259→        ("成功", results["sent"]),
   260→        ("失败", results["failed"]),
   261→        ("跳过(已沟通)", results["skipped"]),
   262→        ("限流", results["rate_limited"]),
   263→    ]
   264→
   265→    for r_idx, (key, value) in enumerate(overview_rows, 1):
   266→        cell_key = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=1, value=key)
   267→        cell_val = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=2, value=value)
   268→        cell_key.font = Font(bold=True)
   269→        for cell in (cell_key, cell_val):
   270→            cell.border = border
   271→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center")
   272→
   273→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["A"].width = 18
   274→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["B"].width = 40
   275→
   276→    # --- Sheet 2: Candidate Details ---
   277→    ws_details = wb.create_sheet(title="候选人详情")
   278→
   279→    columns = ["序号", "姓名", "职位", "公司", "地区", "脉脉ID", "沟通状态", "失败原因"]
   280→    for c_idx, col_name in enumerate(columns, 1):
   281→        cell = ws_details.cell(row=1, column=c_idx, value=col_name)
   282→        cell.font = header_font
   283→        cell.fill = header_fill
   284→        cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center", vertical="center")
   285→        cell.border = border
   286→
   287→    # Build a lookup from maimai_id -> detail
   288→    details_by_id: Dict[str, dict] = {}
   289→    for detail in results.get("details", []):
   290→        details_by_id[detail["maimai_id"]] = detail
   291→
   292→    status_map = {
   293→        "sent": "已发送",
   294→        "failed": "失败",
   295→        "skipped": "跳过",
   296→        "rate_limited": "限流",
   297→    }
   298→
   299→    for r_idx, c in enumerate(candidates, 1):
   300→        detail = details_by_id.get(c.maimai_id, {})
   301→        raw_status = detail.get("status", "not_attempted")
   302→        status_label = status_map.get(raw_status, "未处理")
   303→        reason = detail.get("reason", "")
   304→
   305→        row_data = [r_idx, c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or "", c.maimai_id, status_label, reason]
   306→        for c_idx, value in enumerate(row_data, 1):
   307→            cell = ws_details.cell(row=r_idx + 1, column=c_idx, value=value)
   308→            cell.border = border
   309→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   310→
   311→    detail_widths = {"A": 6, "B": 12, "C": 25, "D": 20, "E": 12, "F": 18, "G": 12, "H": 30}
   312→    for col, width in detail_widths.items():
   313→        ws_details.column_dimensions[col].width = width
   314→
   315→    ws_details.freeze_panes = "A2"
   316→    ws_details.auto_filter.ref = ws_details.dimensions
   317→
   318→    wb.save(str(report_path))
   319→    logger.info(f"Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}")
   320→    console.print(f"[green]Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}[/green]")
   321→    return str(report_path)
   322→

   324→def display_candidates_table(candidates: List[CandidateBasic], title: str) -> None:
   325→    """Display a Rich table of candidates."""
   326→    table = Table(title=f"{title} ({len(candidates)} candidates)")
   327→    table.add_column("#", style="dim", justify="right")
   328→    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
   329→    table.add_column("Title", style="green")
   330→    table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
   331→    table.add_column("Location", style="blue")
   332→    has_scores = any(hasattr(c, '_score') for c in candidates[:1])
   333→    if has_scores:
   334→        table.add_column("Score", style="magenta", justify="right")
   335→    table.add_column("ID", style="dim")
   336→
   337→    for i, c in enumerate(candidates[:50], 1):
   338→        row = [str(i), c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or '']
   339→        if has_scores:
   340→            row.append(str(getattr(c, '_score', '')))
   341→        row.append(c.maimai_id)
   342→        table.add_row(*row)
   343→
   344→    console.print(table)
   345→
   346→    if len(candidates) > 50:
   347→        console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(candidates) - 50} more[/dim]")
   348→
   349→
   350→def display_send_results(results: dict, title: str = "Send Results") -> None:
   351→    """Display send results summary table."""
   352→    console.print()
   353→    result_table = Table(title=title)
   354→    result_table.add_column("Metric", style="bold")
   355→    result_table.add_column("Count", justify="right")
   356→
   357→    result_table.add_row("Total candidates", str(results['total']))
   358→    result_table.add_row("Sent", f"[green]{results['sent']}[/green]")
   359→    result_table.add_row("Failed", f"[red]{results['failed']}[/red]")
   360→    result_table.add_row("Skipped (already messaged)", f"[yellow]{results['skipped']}[/yellow]")
   361→    result_table.add_row("Rate limited", f"[yellow]{results['rate_limited']}[/yellow]")
   362→
   363→    console.print(result_table)
